codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex execution blocked by pretool-tool-policy.py: qa role cannot write to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ because _resolve_path() cannot expand $CODEX_OUT or $OUTFILE to a resolvable path. Three attempts made: (1) with tee $CODEX_OUT — hook sees literal $CODEX_OUT; (2) with redirect to $CODEX_OUT — same block; (3) with redirect to $OUTFILE — same block. This is a live reproduction of the exact bug described in the BA spec. Self-review substituted per graceful fallback clause.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted: 5 adversarial questions answered (over-engineering, under-engineering, edge-case coverage via Form-2 simulation, regression risk, /close-debate readiness). Form-2 spec_text_vs_execution_drift finding surfaced via Python simulation of the AC4 grep recipe.

prior_objections_verification.original_claim: Prior ticket docs/dev/ticket-20260521-085827-fa9f07.md not reconciled — BA claimed no prior attempts.
prior_objections_verification.evidence: ticket-20260521-090100.md Context section (lines 17-18) explicitly names ticket-20260521-085827-fa9f07.md, states it was read, identifies the specific deviation (role count 9 vs correct 11 — missed spec at agents/spec.md:693 and user at agents/user.md:545), and declares supersession. Prior Attempts section (lines 66-78) documents fa9f07 attempt with outcome (wrong_scope — incomplete role enumeration), failure category, and target layer. Resolution is substantive, not nominal.

prior_objections_verification.original_claim: Role count not derivable from agents/*.md — used var/tmp parity heuristic yielding 17 or 12 roles; dev included incorrectly.
prior_objections_verification.evidence: QA independently ran: grep -rn 'Skill(skill="codex")' agents/*.md — 12 hits (architect, ba, cleanliness-inspector, dev, pm, product-owner, prompt-inspector, qa, spec, style-inspector, ui-specialist, user). Technical Hints (line 237) explicitly states 'Do NOT use */var/tmp/codex-outputs/ parity heuristic — that yields 17 roles. Correct list (11): ba, qa, pm, architect, product-owner, user, ui-specialist, spec, prompt-inspector, cleanliness-inspector, style-inspector.' Dev excluded per CR-1.7 (wildcard *). M1 and AC1 both use the 11-role set. The derivation criterion is now grep-evidence-backed and the wrong heuristic is explicitly forbidden.

SKILL.md now appears in: Technical Hints 'Affected files (3)' (third entry); context JSON 'files_to_modify' array; scope_expansion 'additional_found'; component_chain entry (lines 117-126) with line_range 97-133, 'verified_via: Read', and evidence citing lines 103-104 and 112,121,126. M3 requirement explicitly names SKILL.md with identical update requirements to commands/codex.md. AC5 verifies SKILL.md matches commands/codex.md. QA confirmed: SKILL.md exists at /root/.agents/skills/codex/SKILL.md with tee "$CODEX_OUT" at lines 112, 121, 126.

prior_objections_verification.original_claim: AC1 used raw grep count — fragile against _note containing the string.
prior_objections_verification.evidence: AC1 now uses Python set comparison: roles_with_prefix = {r for r, v in data['roles'].items() if '*/docs/codex/' in v.get('allowed_write_path_prefixes', [])} then asserts equality against frozenset {'ba', 'qa', 'pm', 'architect', 'product-owner', 'user', 'ui-specialist', 'spec', 'prompt-inspector', 'cleanliness-inspector', 'style-inspector'}. The acceptance-criteria JSON AC1 check script uses assert roles_with_prefix == expected. This is JSON-semantic: operates only on role entries, not on _note text. No raw grep count. Dev wildcard check also added.

prior_objections_verification.original_claim: AC4 permitted command-substitution tee forms and only checked 1 of 6 tee sites.
prior_objections_verification.evidence: AC4 now has: (a) negative grep for tee "$CODEX_OUT" and tee "${CODEX_OUT}" in BOTH files; (b) enumeration of 3 allowed forms for every physical tee target; (c) explicit prohibition of command-substitution forms tee "$(echo ...)" or tee $(...); (d) allowlist grep check that catches any unrecognized tee target in both files regardless of total tee count. The primary objection (1-of-6) is resolved. However, a NEW spec_text_vs_execution_drift issue is introduced in the revised AC4 — see new_objections.

new_objections.description: AC4 text permits Form 2 as an allowed tee target but the AC4 verifier grep cannot match Form 2.
new_objections.detail: AC4 lists three allowed hook-resolvable tee forms. Form 2 is: '"${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt"' (alternative canonical). The AC4 verifier grep in acceptance-criteria-20260521-090100.json (checks 3 and 4) uses pattern 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex|/var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output'. The string '${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex/...' does NOT contain the substring 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex'. Python verification confirms: re.search('CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex', '${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt') returns None. The string contains 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex' — the '/' before docs is preceded by '}' not 'DIR'. Therefore if dev uses Form 2 (which BA explicitly permits in AC4 text lines 175-176), the allowlist grep check returns count=1 (non-zero) and AC4 fails incorrectly. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift: AC4 literal text permits Form 2 but the only runnable verifier rejects it.
files_affected: docs/dev/ticket-20260521-090100.md (AC4 text, lines 174-178, the enumeration of 3 allowed forms)
new_objections.proposed_fix: BA must choose one of two remedies and update both the ticket AC4 text and the acceptance-criteria JSON: (A) Remove Form 2 from AC4's allowed list and from M4 — keep only Form 1 ($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/...) and Form 3 (fallback literal /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-$$-...). The Technical Hints tee restructuring section (lines 228-232) already shows only Form 1 in the example, so dev guidance is already consistent with this. (B) Update the AC4 verifier grep pattern to also match Form 2: change 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex' to match 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR[^/]*/docs/codex' or use a two-pattern OR. Option A (removal of Form 2) is simpler and eliminates the ambiguity with no loss of functionality since dev is guided toward Form 1.
new_objections.per_r6_rule: Per agents/qa.md R6 reminder (task 20260519-211515, dual-anchor occurrence #1 and #2): 'if dev verification recipe differs from AC literal text, raise spec_text_vs_execution_drift regardless of equivalence judgment. This is a blocking BA-validation objection; verdict MUST be FAIL.' The AC4 text says Form 2 is allowed; the verifier grep rejects it. This is the drift pattern and is blocking.

evidence_quality.notes: All factual claims have file-read evidence: bash_write_targets.py:152-170 read confirmed, commands/codex.md tee sites at 97/106/111 read confirmed, SKILL.md tee sites at 112/121/126 read confirmed, policy JSON read confirmed. Codex runtime test (extract_bash_write_paths) cited with specific invocation and result. Agent grep for Skill(skill="codex") is reproducible and QA independently verified 12 hits. Root cause diagnosis is precise (variable non-expansion, not path absence).

scope_alignment.notes: User verbatim requirement: terminate all subagent no-tee workarounds. BA covers all 6 physical tee sites in both files, all 11 relevant roles (correctly derived from grep evidence), policy_version tracking. Won't Have exclusions have explicit user authorization (do-eventually classification). Dev exclusion per CR-1.7 is correct. No narrowing of the universal claim without user authorization.

investigation_completeness.notes: Prior ticket fa9f07 reconciled with specific deviation documented and justified. Grep investigation of agents/*.md completed. bash_write_targets.py source read confirmed. All 6 tee sites identified and mapped. policy_version baseline confirmed as 3. Codex runtime verification cited.

affected_file_accuracy.notes: All three affected files exist and contain the claimed content per independent QA verification. policies/tool-policy.v1.json: policy_version=3, zero roles with */docs/codex/. commands/codex.md: 3 tee sites at lines 97/106/111 using tee "$CODEX_OUT". /root/.agents/skills/codex/SKILL.md: 3 tee sites at lines 112/121/126 using tee "$CODEX_OUT". No phantom files.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: NEW-OBJ-1 is a blocking drift issue in AC4. AC4 text permits Form 2 tee target (${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex/...) but the AC4 verifier grep pattern 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex' does not match that string. If dev uses Form 2 (explicitly permitted by BA), AC4 incorrectly fails. Per R6 this is a blocking BA-validation objection.

summary: FAIL (iteration 2). All 5 prior blocking objections are resolved with adequate evidence. However, a new spec_text_vs_execution_drift issue is introduced in AC4: the alternative canonical tee Form 2 (${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex/...) is listed as an allowed tee target in AC4 text and in M4, but the AC4 acceptance-criteria verifier grep pattern 'CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex' does not match it (the '/' before 'docs' is preceded by ':-$PWD}' not by 'DIR'). If dev uses Form 2, the allowlist grep check returns a non-zero count and AC4 fails incorrectly. Per agents/qa.md R6 (task 20260519-211515, dual-anchor), spec_text_vs_execution_drift is a blocking BA-validation objection. Resolution is simple: either remove Form 2 from AC4's allowed list (consistent with Technical Hints tee restructuring hint which only shows Form 1) or update the verifier grep pattern to match Form 2. Note: Codex execution was blocked by the qa role tool-policy restriction — live reproduction of the exact bug this spec fixes — confirming the bug is real and the fix is necessary.
verdict_reasoning: All 5 prior objections resolved. 1 new blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift objection in AC4 forces FAIL per R6 rule. This is a single targeted fix; BA should be able to resolve it in iteration 3 with minimal rework.
